title: Arasaleeswarar temple
tagline: Arasaleeswarar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Arasaleeswarar Temple (also called Arasili) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Olindiyampattu, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of
overview.description: Arasaleeswarar Temple (also called Arasili) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Olindiyampattu, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Arasaleeswarar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Periyanaayagi. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Kottakarai
topPlaces.description: Kottakarai is an Indian village in Villupuram district of Tamil Nadu. The village is called Kottakarai because kottai means palace in English.

topPlaces.name: Irumbai
topPlaces.description: Irumbai is a village situated next to Auroville in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and at about 10 km from Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Vanur block
topPlaces.description: The Vanur block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 65 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Vanur taluk
topPlaces.description: Vanur taluk is a taluk of Viluppuram district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Vanur.

topPlaces.name: Matrimandir
topPlaces.description: The Matrimandir is an edifice of spiritual significance for practitioners of integral yoga in the centre of Auroville, India established by Mirra Alfassa of the Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Auroville's spiritual co-founder, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère. It is called Soul of the City (French: L'âme de la Ville) and situated in a large open space called "Paix" (French for Peace).

topPlaces.name: Auroville
topPlaces.description: Auroville (; City of Dawn French: Cité de l'aube) is an experimental township in Viluppuram district, mostly in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, with some parts in the Union Territory of Puducherry in India. It was founded in 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (known as "the Mother" or "La Mère") and designed by architect Roger Anger.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Puducherry
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Puducherry or locally known as JNV Kalapet is a boarding, co-educational school in Puducherry district of Puducherry U.T. in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

topPlaces.name: Ganapathichettikulam
topPlaces.description: Ganapathichettikulam is a fishing hamlet, along with Periyakalapet, Chinnakalapet and Pillaichavad, next to Kalapet near Pondicherry on the south eastern coast of India. It is a ward in the municipality of Oulgaret.

items.note: Kottakarai is an Indian village in Villupuram district of Tamil Nadu. The village is called Kottakarai because kottai means palace in English.
Irumbai is a village situated next to Auroville in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and at about 10 km from Pondicherry.
The Vanur block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 65 panchayat villages.
Vanur taluk is a taluk of Viluppuram district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Vanur.

The Matrimandir is an edifice of spiritual significance for practitioners of integral yoga in the centre of Auroville, India established by Mirra Alfassa of the Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Auroville's spiritual co-founder, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère. It is called Soul of the City (French: L'âme de la Ville) and situated in a large open space called "Paix" (French for Peace).

Auroville (; City of Dawn French: Cité de l'aube) is an experimental township in Viluppuram district, mostly in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, with some parts in the Union Territory of Puducherry in India. It was founded in 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (known as "the Mother" or "La Mère") and designed by architect Roger Anger.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Puducherry & around

items.note: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Puducherry or locally known as JNV Kalapet is a boarding, co-educational school in Puducherry district of Puducherry U.T. in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

Ganapathichettikulam is a fishing hamlet, along with Periyakalapet, Chinnakalapet and Pillaichavad, next to Kalapet near Pondicherry on the south eastern coast of India. It is a ward in the municipality of Oulgaret.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Arasaleeswarar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Arasaleeswarar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Arasaleeswarar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (113 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Arasaleeswarar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Arasaleeswarar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Arasaleeswarar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Arasaleeswarar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Arasaleeswarar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kottakarai, Irumbai, Vanur block, Vanur taluk, Matrimandir.
